rationale: Biology has the most diverse career paths of any level-1 — a molecular biologist and a marine biologist barely speak the same language. Sixteen subspecialties balance two organizing principles: scale (molecular → cellular → organismal → systems) and topic (immunology, developmental, etc.). I deliberately did NOT separate plant biology from animal biology — model organism choice is methodology, not subspecialty. I kept Microbiology distinct from Immunology even though they overlap heavily because the professional identities are sharply different (microbiology departments, immunology departments). I demoted Cancer Biology into its own card because basic-science cancer mechanism is enormous in volume and identity, and I do NOT want it to crowd Oncology under Medicine. Bioinformatics gets its own card despite being a method, because the community treats it as an identity; same for Structural Biology.

subspecialties.name: Molecular Biology
subspecialties.tagline: DNA, RNA, proteins — transcription, translation, regulation.
covers: transcription, translation, RNA biology, chromatin, gene regulation, protein structure-function, signaling cascades at molecular level

subspecialties.name: Cell Biology
covers: cell cycle, organelle biology, cytoskeleton, membrane trafficking, autophagy, apoptosis, cell signaling at cellular level
JCB, Nat Cell Biol, Mol Biol Cell

subspecialties.name: Genetics & Genomics
subspecialties.tagline: Inheritance, GWAS, functional genomics, population genetics.
covers: population genetics, statistical genetics, GWAS, functional genomics, epigenomics, comparative genomics, human genetics (research)
Nat Genet, AJHG, Genome Res

subspecialties.name: Developmental Biology
covers: embryology, organogenesis, regenerative biology, stem cell biology, developmental signaling, evo-devo
Development, Dev Cell, Stem Cell Rep

subspecialties.name: Microbiology
covers: bacterial physiology, microbial ecology, microbiome research, virology (basic), antimicrobial resistance mechanism, phages, archaea, mycology
mBio, Cell Host Microbe, Nat Microbiol

subspecialties.name: Immunology
subspecialties.tagline: Innate, adaptive, immune cell biology, host-pathogen.
covers: innate immunity, adaptive immunity, T/B cell biology, antigen presentation, immunometabolism, tumor immunology basic, host-pathogen at immune level
Nat Immunol, Immunity, J Exp Med

subspecialties.name: Cancer Biology
covers: oncogenes, tumor suppressors, metastasis biology, tumor microenvironment, cancer stem cells, basic immuno-oncology, cancer metabolism
Cancer Discov, Cancer Cell, Nat Cancer

subspecialties.name: Biochemistry
covers: enzymology, metabolism, lipid biology, glycobiology, biophysical chemistry of biomolecules, chemical biology, drug-target biochemistry
Biochemistry, JBC, FEBS J

subspecialties.name: Structural Biology
covers: X-ray crystallography, cryo-EM, NMR structure determination, computational structural biology, AlphaFold/structure prediction, membrane protein structure
Nature Struct Mol Biol, Structure
explicitly_excludes: protein structure prediction as ML methods → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Bioinformatics & Computational Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Sequence analysis, single-cell, pipelines, ML for biology.
covers: sequence analysis, single-cell methods, multi-omics integration, biological databases, ML applied to biology, computational pipelines
Bioinformatics, Nat Methods, Genome Biol
explicitly_excludes: pure ML method development → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Systems Biology & Synthetic Biology
covers: gene regulatory networks, metabolic modeling, synthetic gene circuits, design-build-test cycles, whole-cell modeling, CRISPR-based design

subspecialties.name: Plant Biology
covers: plant cell biology, plant molecular biology, plant-pathogen interaction, photosynthesis, plant development, plant signaling
Plant Cell, Plant J, Nat Plants
explicitly_excludes: crop improvement and agronomy → Agriculture & Food

subspecialties.name: Physiology
covers: cardiovascular, renal, respiratory, GI, endocrine physiology at organism level, integrative physiology, exercise physiology, comparative physiology
explicitly_excludes: neurophysiology of behavior → Brain & Cognitive Sciences

subspecialties.name: Biophysics
covers: single-molecule biophysics, membrane biophysics, molecular dynamics simulations, force spectroscopy, fluorescence techniques, biophysical theory
Biophys J, eLife biophysics papers

subspecialties.name: Marine & Aquatic Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Ocean life, freshwater systems, aquatic ecology overlap.
covers: marine organisms, marine microbiology, coral biology, freshwater biology, fish biology (non-aquaculture), aquatic toxicology

subspecialties.name: Neurobiology
subspecialties.tagline: Neuron and glia biology, synapse, brain at cellular level.
covers: cellular and molecular neuroscience, synaptic biology, glia biology, neurodevelopment, neuroinflammation at cellular level
Neuron, J Neurosci basic, Nat Neurosci basic
explicitly_excludes: systems and cognitive neuroscience → Brain & Cognitive Sciences

subspecialties.name: Zoology & Organismal Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Invertebrate, vertebrate, comparative animal biology — behavior to morphology.
covers: invertebrate zoology, vertebrate zoology, comparative animal physiology, animal behavior, functional and evolutionary morphology, entomology, herpetology, ornithology, mammalogy
explicitly_excludes: whole-ecosystem and population work → Ecology & Evolution

uncovered_concerns: Considered separating Virology from Microbiology — virology IS distinct and has its own departments. Kept merged because both are <8% individually and the merged card better matches how undergrads/grad students self-identify.
Considered splitting RNA biology from Molecular Biology — RNA-as-identity is rising fast (after the mRNA vaccine moment). Marked for v2.
Considered Toxicology — it's an applied field that lands in Public Health (env-tox), Pharmacology (drug-tox), and Medicine (clinical-tox). Not a level-2 in Biology.
Considered Pharmacology — it sits at Biology+Chemistry+Medicine border. Most basic pharmacology papers fit under Biochemistry; clinical pharmacology fits under Medicine.
